behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (diff parser -> review-input builder). A unit test of one helper could miss scoping loss across that boundary (MOCK-MISS). Real OS/network is not required by this behavior text, so System is rejected. LLM output quality is not part of this behavior, so Agentic/Workflow are rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real local modules with a fixture diff containing both changed and unchanged hunks; assert the final review payload includes only changed-line snippets and excludes unchanged file regions.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Correct diff anchoring depends on real PR diff-position semantics and API behavior (REAL-INFRA, MOCK-HIDE). In-process mocks often accept invalid position/path combinations that GitHub rejects or remaps. Agentic is rejected because this behavior is about placement mechanics, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using produced anchors and verify each appears on the intended file/line in the actual diff UI/API thread location.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on real model judgment quality (LLM-DEP). Stubbed findings would always pass and hide the failure. Workflow was considered but rejected because the behavior text requires a single outcome ('identifies at least one real defect'), not correctness of multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run analysis with the real model on a PR fixture containing a seeded bug; assert at least one returned finding correctly points to that bug (or equivalent true defect) with acceptable evidence.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module state consistency (inline post results -> summary assembler). A single-function unit test can miss ID mapping regressions across components (MOCK-MISS). System was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require real network semantics, only same-run correspondence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute posting and summary-building modules together with deterministic posted-comment responses; assert every summary entry references an ID produced in that run and no foreign/stale IDs appear.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in pipeline handoff/aggregation between inline findings and summary payload, not necessarily within one pure function. No real OS/network or model judgment is required by the behavior text, so System/Agentic/Workflow are rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Run local pipeline stages from findings collection through summary payload construction; assert cardinality and IDs/content parity between inline findings and summary payload.

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a real pull request and live API interactions (REAL-INFRA). Integration with fakes can miss auth, API contract, ordering, and posting edge cases. Agentic is rejected because findings are precomputed (no model-output dependence).
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end against a real PR using fixed findings; verify diff read succeeds, inline comments are posted, summary posts successfully, and run exits without manual steps.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is a multi-step pipeline with model-generated decisions plus real external side effects (MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP + REAL-INFRA). Single agentic-call tests cannot catch sequencing/handoff failures across analyze -> inline post -> summary post.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full unattended pipeline on a real PR with real model generation; assert successful completion and coherent artifacts across stages (findings, inline posts, final summary linkage).
